title: APIs: 3GL Functions
text: APIs: 3GL Functions Unless otherwise indicated, all 3GL functions are available on all platforms. In the 3GL function descriptions, note the following: All Uniface 3GL functions have lowercase names, as do most of the symbols in the shared libraries. However, there are some exceptions. The include file h3gl.h maps the lowercase names to uppercase or to mixed-case, if necessary. We recommend that you always include h3gl.h , and call functions using only their lowercase names. If you do not include h3gl.h , you might get unresolved externals when you link in the shared library. String parameters for the functions, input or output, are always zero-terminated, C style, and passed by reference as a pointer, unless otherwise noted. Instead of saying the precisely correct, format —a pointer to a zero-terminated character string containing the format, we simply say: format —a string containing the format, or the format.. For brevity, most of the examples shown omit the checking of return codes. Always check against all possible documented values, and take the applicable action. This is a good programming practice, which we strongly endorse. Note: Functions that are not mentioned in this reference material are not supported and should not be used. A number of Uniface 3GL functions were documented in previous versions of Uniface, but are now obsolete. These functions are listed in the "Obsolete 3GL Functions" topic. Some of these functions are mentioned in the include files that we distribute in the include directory of your Uniface installation. However, this does not imply that you can use them. Related Links Obsolete 3GL Functions Some Uniface 3GL functions that were previously available or documented are obsolete. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
